soliton.text: Regex character class `_[a-zA-Z-_]*\.properties` uses `-` in a way that creates an unintended ASCII range (`Z-_`) instead of a literal dash, which can cause incorrect locale-suffix stripping and wrong English file resolution
Locale-suffix stripping regex uses `*` instead of `+`, allowing an empty suffix so a filename like `_.properties` would incorrectly match
`file.getAbsolutePath().replaceAll("resources-community", "resources")` performs an unanchored substring replacement that can rewrite unintended parts of the path and resolve the wrong `englishFile`
`verifySafeHtml()` catches `IOException` and rethrows `RuntimeException`, which is inconsistent with the caller’s `MojoExecutionException` handling and leads to unhelpful Maven failure reporting

Fields `POLICY_SOME_HTML`, `POLICY_NO_HTML`, and `HTML_TAGS` are named like constants but are non-static/non-final and not private, causing repeated rebuilding per instance and inconsistent style with other constants
Method name `santizeAnchors` is misspelled; should be `sanitizeAnchors` at both declaration and call sites

AccessTokenContext constructor mistakenly calls requireNonNull(grantType) twice and never validates rawTokenId, allowing null rawTokenId to propagate and produce token IDs like 'onrtac:null' that collide/break downstream operations
AssertEvents.isAccessTokenId test matcher uses wrong substring indices (substring(3,5) instead of reading grant shortcut at [4:6]) causing incorrect parsing of the encoded prefix

AssertEvents.isAccessTokenId test matcher has inverted boolean logic (returns false when the grant shortcut matches), making grant-type assertions silently ineffective and allowing wrong shortcuts to pass tests

DefaultTokenContextEncoderProvider dereferences userSession without a null-check; clientSessionContext.getClientSession().getUserSession() can be null for detached sessions, causing NPEs in token issuance hot path

DefaultTokenContextEncoderProviderFactory duplicate-shortcut detection is flawed because ConcurrentHashMap.put overwrites existing entries before size-based collision checks, allowing shortcut collisions to go undetected and later fail decoding
OAuth2GrantTypeFactory SPI adds abstract getShortcut() without a default implementation, causing binary incompatibility and AbstractMethodError at startup for third-party/out-of-tree implementations

OAuth2GrantType.Context copy constructor was removed from public SPI without replacement, causing NoSuchMethodError/runtime linkage failures (and compile failures) for external extensions that clone/wrap Context

DefaultTokenContextEncoderProvider token-id decoding handles legacy tokens gracefully but throws IllegalArgumentException for unknown encoded shortcuts, breaking rolling upgrades/heterogeneous clusters by turning unknown prefixes into HTTP 500s

DefaultTokenContextEncoderProvider.getTokenContextFromTokenId throws on attacker-controlled token input and includes the full raw token ID in exception messages, risking HTTP 500s and sensitive/log injection (CWE-209)

DefaultTokenContextEncoderProviderFactory lazy shortcut-map refresh updates two ConcurrentHashMaps without synchronization, allowing races that break the bijection invariant between grantsByShortcuts and grantsToShortcuts and can miss collisions

AbstractOIDCProtocolMapper changed getShouldUseLightweightToken from instance method to public static, so subclass overrides are silently ignored (static hiding), causing behavioral regressions without compile-time errors

CredentialHelper.createRecoveryCodesCredential serializes and sends raw plaintext recovery codes to user-storage providers via updateCredential, causing cleartext persistence/logging risk for long-lived bearer secrets
RecoveryAuthnCodeInputLoginBean calls Optional.get() without checking presence when no recovery credential exists, leading to NoSuchElementException and a 500 during login form rendering

CredentialHelper stores a JSON array of raw List<String> as credentialData, but RecoveryAuthnCodesCredentialModel.createFromCredentialModel expects hashed RecoveryAuthnCodesCredentialData JSON, causing deserialization/type mismatch and potential NPE/auth failures
BackwardsCompatibilityUserStorage.isValid validates recovery codes without invalidating/removing the used code, allowing infinite replay of one-time recovery codes

CredentialHelper hard-codes the provider ID string "keycloak-recovery-authn-codes" instead of using RecoveryAuthnCodesCredentialProviderFactory.PROVIDER_ID, risking drift and null provider/NPE if the ID changes

CredentialHelper fallback logic misinterprets updateCredential’s boolean return (handled vs not handled), which can cause double-writes to federated+local stores or skipping creation of the properly formed local credential

RecoveryAuthnCodesUtils.getCredential prefers federated credentials over local without warning, so after adding/migrating to a federated provider a stale/absent federated credential can silently shadow a valid local credential and lock users out
CLI exit-code contract is silently broken by changing RECREATE_UPGRADE_EXIT_CODE from 4 to 3 and reusing 4 for FEATURE_DISABLED, which can cause existing automation to misinterpret outcomes
No deprecation/upgrade-guide documentation is provided for the external-facing exit-code change between releases
UpdateCommandDistTest.testFeatureNotEnabled asserts only the error message and does not verify the exit code, leaving the new numeric exit-code contract untested and prone to regression

Custom-image Dockerfile unconditionally bakes --features=rolling-updates into kc.sh build, potentially enabling a preview feature by default for downstream derived images and contradicting the intended gating unless clearly test-only/documented

ClientRemovedEvent cleanup calls ClientPermissionsV2.setPermissionsEnabled(client, false), which unconditionally throws UnsupportedOperationException in V2, potentially aborting client deletion transactions

AdminPermissions.registerListener wraps role/client/group removal cleanup in a single ADMIN_FINE_GRAINED_AUTHZ feature-flag guard, causing cleanup to stop running for other fine-grained-authz flag variants and leading to orphan Resource/Policy records

ClientPermissionsV2.hasPermission(ClientModel, String) fallback path can pass a null wildcard resource from AdminPermissionsSchema.getResourceTypeResource(...) into policyStore.findByResource(...), causing a NullPointerException when the wildcard resource is missing

ClientPermissionsV2.getClientsWithPermission omits clients that are authorized only via the wildcard "Clients" resource because it only iterates resources returned by findByType (per-client resources), leading to under-reported authorized client sets

ClientPermissionsV2 canMapRoles/canMapCompositeRoles/canMapClientScopeRoles lack the MANAGE_CLIENTS admin-role fast-path present in V1, causing admins with only MANAGE_CLIENTS to be denied role-mapping in V2 unless explicit MAP_ROLES policies exist

ClientPermissionsV2 canView(ClientScopeModel)/canManage(ClientScopeModel) reuse the Clients resource type instead of a dedicated ClientScopes resource type, unintentionally broadening permissions so principals with client view/manage can also view/manage all client scopes
GroupPermissionsV2.canManage() incorrectly treats having only the VIEW scope on the all-groups resource as sufficient to pass a MANAGE check, enabling read-to-write privilege escalation
getGroupIdsWithViewPermission adds authorization Resource UUIDs (resource.getId()) instead of group model IDs (resource.getName()/group.getId()), breaking downstream consumers that expect group IDs

Because getGroupIdsWithViewPermission returns Resource UUIDs, hasPermission(groupResource.getId(), ...) looks up by name using a UUID, misses the specific-group resource, and silently falls back to type-level (all-groups) permissions, weakening per-group authorization checks

UsersResource.searchForUser removed the per-user usersEvaluator.canView post-filter and relies only on an optional GROUPS session attribute, allowing QUERY_USERS-only callers with no view permissions to enumerate all users when groupIds is empty
BruteForceUsersResource.searchForUser has the same missing canView post-filter pattern as UsersResource, creating the same user-enumeration/authorization bypass risk

UserPermissionsV2 dropped AdminRoles.ADMIN from the canView/canManage fast-path checks, potentially causing admins with only the ADMIN role (without composite grants or fine-grained policies) to receive 403s where they previously had access

AdminPermissions.registerListener now registers the listener only if the feature flag is enabled at registration time, so enabling the feature later (tests/profile reload) would skip cleanup events and leave orphaned permission resources in the database
ModelToRepresentation.searchGroupModelsByAttributes was removed as a public static method, creating a binary-incompatible change for downstream extensions/providers that depend on it
Adding abstract method order() to public CryptoProvider SPI breaks source/binary compatibility for third-party implementations (can cause compile failures or AbstractMethodError at runtime)
AuthzClientCryptoProvider.getEcdsaCryptoProvider().concatenatedRSToASN1DER contains dead allocations: two ASN1Encoder instances write BigIntegers but their results are discarded

CryptoIntegration.detectProvider behavior changed from fail-fast on multiple providers to silently selecting by highest order(), which can hide misconfiguration and lead to non-deterministic provider selection when orders tie

ASN1Decoder.readLength returns -1 for indefinite-length encoding and callers pass it to new byte[length], causing NegativeArraySizeException instead of a clear IOException; DER should reject indefinite length explicitly

ECDSAAlgorithmTest lacks negative/malformed-input tests for the new hand-rolled ASN.1 parser (e.g., invalid tags, high-tag-form, indefinite/oversized lengths, wrong element count, length mismatches, truncated inputs)
GroupAdapter.getSubGroupsCount() now returns null when modelSupplier.get() returns null, violating the non-null expectation of the GroupModel.getSubGroupsCount() contract

Returning null from getSubGroupsCount() can trigger NullPointerException in callers that auto-unbox to primitive long, use the value in arithmetic, or pass it to serializers/setters that call longValue()
GroupTest.java reader thread lacks a timeout/join, risking hanging/flaky tests

Possible compile error: UsernameForm.authenticate() calls isConditionalPasskeysEnabled() with zero arguments, but only a one-argument overload (isConditionalPasskeysEnabled(UserModel user)) is defined/visible in the PR; should likely pass context.getUser() or ensure a zero-arg overload exists in the superclass chain.
Unused helper method `_create_process_for_shard(self, shard: int)` is defined but never called, risking drift and dead code
Metric tag key for the shard dimension is inconsistent (`shard` vs `shards`) across related metrics, fragmenting dashboards/alerts
Metric tag value shape for shard is inconsistent (single-shard integer vs comma-joined multi-shard string) across metrics, causing inconsistent aggregation
`SpanFlusher.__init__` accepts a caller-supplied `SpansBuffer` but effectively discards it (only reads `assigned_shards`) and constructs new per-process buffers instead

Creating new per-process buffers and pickling them into child processes multiplies Redis client/state (up to ~3 copies per shard group) and silently drops any initialization state on the caller-provided buffer

OptimizedCursorPaginator.get_result can perform negative Django ORM slicing when cursor.is_prev is true, causing an unconditional AssertionError crash and enabling a DoS via attacker-controlled cursor.offset
Inline comments incorrectly claim Django QuerySets handle negative slicing/boundary conditions automatically, which is false and may mislead maintainers
OptimizedCursorPaginator.get_item_key calls math.floor/math.ceil on a datetime field (from order_by="-datetime"), raising TypeError and crashing cursor construction for audit log pagination
organization_auditlogs.py dereferences organization_context.member.has_global_access without a None check, causing AttributeError when member is None (e.g., non-member user)
Authorization gate incorrectly uses member.has_global_access as a privilege check, allowing ordinary members (e.g., open-membership orgs) to access the advanced/optimized audit log path
BasePaginator.get_result clamps negative forward offsets to 0, silently changing behavior and masking cursor-corruption bugs that previously failed loudly; should be documented and/or logged
Comments in cursors.py/paginator.py falsely claim negative offsets enable efficient reverse/bidirectional pagination; this is incorrect and promotes a broken approach
OptimizedCursorPaginator docstring falsely advertises optimized/boundary-handling behavior despite the implementation being a near-copy with crashing branches, misleading future maintainers

Lock narrowing in bleveBackend.BuildIndex introduces a TOCTOU race where concurrent calls for the same key can both build an index and the second overwrites/discards the first, causing wasted work and incorrect behavior
Concurrent same-key BuildIndex can double-increment metrics (IndexMetrics.IndexTenants) because both goroutines run the full build path
For file-backed indexes, concurrent builds for the same key can target the same directory and cause bleve.New to fail/open duplicate handles, potentially leaving orphaned/corrupted on-disk state
NewResourceServer now runs synchronous index initialization under the caller-provided context, so caller deadlines/cancellation can abort startup and make construction fail

Removing per-method Init guards means callers that ignore NewResourceServer errors or construct the server directly (e.g., in tests) lose the previous lazy-init safety net and may use an uninitialized server

MetricAlertDetectorHandler now subclasses StatefulDetectorHandler but implements none of its required abstract methods (get_dedupe_value, get_group_key_values, build_occurrence_and_event_data), so instantiation raises TypeError and breaks metric-alert detector evaluation

process_detectors assumes handler.evaluate() returns a dict and calls .values(); any DetectorHandler subclass still returning a list will crash at runtime with AttributeError ('list' object has no attribute values')
Dataclass field `queued` uses `timezone.now()` as a bare default, so it’s evaluated once at import time and shared across instances instead of being set per-instance
`to_dict()` returns a `datetime` in the payload, which is not JSON-serializable for Celery’s default JSON serializer and can raise a `TypeError` when enqueueing the task

`from_dict()` can accept a serialized datetime string and pass it into the `queued: datetime` field without parsing, silently producing an `AssignmentSource` where `queued` is a `str` and violating the intended type contract

`installation.should_sync()` is called with a new second positional argument, which will raise `TypeError` for integration subclasses that still implement the old 1-parameter `should_sync(self, attribute)` signature
Uses Python 3.13-only APIs (queue.ShutDown exception and Queue.shutdown()), causing AttributeError on Python <= 3.12 and preventing queues from closing
On Python <= 3.12, workers can remain blocked forever on work_queue.get() during shutdown, causing join timeouts and daemon-thread teardown mid-processing (dropping in-flight work)
FixedQueuePool.shutdown() closes/cancels workers without draining already-submitted items, leading to abandoned work and stalled offset tracking
wait_until_empty() is not invoked from close()/terminate()/shutdown paths, so graceful shutdown does not drain queues before stopping
Offsets are added to OffsetTracker before enqueueing; abandoned items remain outstanding so the commit loop cannot advance past them
Shutdown behavior can cause Kafka to re-read offsets on restart, resulting in duplicate processing of queued items after deploys
OffsetTracker dictionaries keyed by Partition (all_offsets/outstanding/last_committed/partition_locks) are never evicted on partition revocation, causing unbounded state/lock growth over time
FixedQueuePool.submit() marks offsets as added before the item is safely enqueued; if put()/grouping/hash raises, offsets may become committable even though work never executed
_commit_loop has a shutdown race where setting shutdown_event can cause the loop to exit without a final get_committable_offsets()/commit pass, dropping the last bit of progress
OptimizedCursorPaginator uses a negative cursor.offset as the start of Django QuerySet slicing, triggering AssertionError and crashing paginated requests
BasePaginator.get_result allows negative start_offset when cursor.is_prev is true, enabling a crafted prev-cursor to crash any paginated endpoint via Django QuerySet negative slicing (DoS)
Lua add-buffer script uses ZUNIONSTORE without specifying AGGREGATE MAX, so default SUM inflates scores for spans present in both sets and corrupts eviction ordering
Cursor.__init__ no longer validates non-negative offsets, allowing attacker-controlled cursor strings to inject large negative offsets into paginator logic

Audit-log endpoint’s authorization gate for optimized pagination is too permissive (allows has_global_access members), enabling non-staff users to trigger the advanced pagination path and associated risks
_load_segment_data removed the max_segment_spans guard, eliminating defense-in-depth/telemetry and allowing oversized segments to be loaded without bounds if Lua-side eviction is bypassed
Git submodule `sentry-repo` is added as a gitlink but `.gitmodules` is not updated, so `git submodule update --init`/CI checkouts cannot resolve it

`_is_error_focused_query` uses a naive substring check (`"event.type:error" in query.lower()`), misclassifying negated/compound/quoted/unrelated queries as error-focused and rewriting them to `upsampled_count()` incorrectly
60-second allowlist decision cache can keep upsampling enabled for up to 60 seconds after a project is removed from the allowlist because cache invalidation is not wired to option changes

Allowlist cache key uses `hash(tuple(...))`, which is process-local/unstable for non-int tuple contents and can cause inconsistent keys across workers (defeating caching) if `project_ids` ever include non-ints
`_set_sample_rate_from_error_sampling` uses `except Exception: pass`, silently swallowing unexpected errors and potentially causing tests to skip setting `sample_rate` without noticing
An unrelated user-facing typo/message fix is mixed into the feature PR, increasing diff noise and complicating reverts/review
`transform_query_columns_for_error_upsampling` is redundantly called in multiple branches; it should be computed once to avoid duplication and the risk of missing a branch

`upsampled_count` assumes `sample_weight` is non-null for all rows; NULL `sample_weight` values will be skipped by `sum`, causing silent undercounting unless handled (e.g., `ifNull`/`sumIf`) or the ingestion invariant is enforced and tested

Denial-cache key construction in userPermDenialCacheKey uses '_' as a delimiter even though components may contain '_', causing cache-key collisions between different (action,name,parent) tuples and incorrect denied results
Permission denial cache entries are never invalidated/purged when permissions are later granted, so previously denied resources can remain inaccessible until shortCacheTTL expires
permissionCacheUsage("false") metric is now emitted for cache-hit-but-denied cases as well as true cache misses, silently changing metric semantics and inflating the 'false' counter

Check treats any error from getCachedIdentityPermissions as a cache miss, swallowing real lookup/identifier errors (e.g., GetUserIdentifiers failures or unsupported identity type), causing redundant failing work and misattributed metrics/logging
Uncaught HTTPError (e.g., 401 Unauthorized) from get_user_info() propagates and crashes the OAuth install flow with a 500 instead of showing a graceful error page
Direct access integration.metadata["sender"]["login"] can raise KeyError when metadata is missing or differently shaped, causing a 500 (and potential info disclosure) instead of failing gracefully
Race condition: OAuth callback may arrive before the installation webhook persists integration.metadata["sender"]["login"], causing legitimate installs to fail (invalid request or KeyError)
Installer identity is derived from webhook-persisted metadata instead of querying GitHub (e.g., /app/installations/{id}), making the authorization check unreliable under race/jitter
Removing FORWARD_INSTALL_FOR from pipeline_advancer.py can break external importers that still import this constant, causing ImportError at import time
OAuth state uses pipeline.signature (not a fresh per-request nonce), allowing replay within the pipeline lifetime and weakening CSRF protection
OAuth state comparison uses plain != instead of constant-time comparison (e.g., hmac.compare_digest), enabling potential timing side-channel leakage
Test test_github_user_mismatch mutates the webhook body but uses a hardcoded HMAC signature that won’t match the mutated payload, so webhook validation will fail and the test will break
installation_id can be overwritten via request.GET on the OAuth callback leg, enabling a mid-flow installation swap and undermining the intended identity-binding check
Token exchange wraps parsing in a bare except that silently swallows real failures (network/parse/programming errors) by setting payload = {}, making incidents hard to diagnose without logging
Test cleanup registers literal "alias" instead of the loop variable when removing created IDPs, so cleanup no-ops and orphaned IDPs leak across test runs causing flaky count assertions
Later creation of "idp-alias-20" also lacks correct cleanup removal registration, further leaking test-created IDPs

In getForLogin(), cache-hit path returns raw/unwrapped IdentityProviderModel while cache-miss/fallback returns createOrganizationAwareIdentityProviderModel-wrapped models, causing inconsistent behavior between cache hits and misses

Login cache invalidation is not triggered when IDP↔organization membership changes via OrganizationProvider add/removeIdentityProvider (without going through IdentityProviderStorageProvider.update), leaving stale cached login IDP sets
getLoginPredicate() adds a clause that dereferences idp.getConfig() without a null check, risking NPE if getConfig() can be null
getLoginPredicate() must remain aligned with JpaIdentityProviderStorageProvider.getForLogin() DB filtering; misalignment could cause cache invalidation to miss relevant IDPs and leave stale entries
Test scenario 4 conflates update() and addIdentityProvider(), so it cannot verify that addIdentityProvider alone triggers cache invalidation
Tests lack coverage for cache invalidation when removing a login-eligible IDP (current removal test uses a disabled/non-eligible IDP), so regressions in remove-path invalidation could go undetected
enableSqlExpressions always returns false because both branches return false, making the feature-flag lookup have no effect
enableSqlExpressions assigns enabled to the negation of the flag state, creating inverted/misleading control flow that could be “simplified” incorrectly and re-enable the vulnerable path
No explicit comment documents that SQL expressions are intentionally disabled as a mitigation for CVE-2024-9264, increasing risk of accidental re-enablement
Missing regression test to ensure SQL expressions remain disabled even if FlagSqlExpressions is set, so CI won’t catch accidental re-enablement

pkg/expr/sql/parser.go retains a vulnerable fmt.Sprintf-based SQL string interpolation pattern with naive quote escaping, which is unsafe for DuckDB and could reintroduce CVE-2024-9264 if the stub is replaced with a real engine
TablesList should early-return an error instead of keeping dormant vulnerable SQL-building code that could become executable later
Error messages like "sqlExpressions is not implemented" / "not implemented" are misleading because the feature is intentionally disabled for CVE-2024-9264 and provide no actionable/security context
ReadQuery/DB stub paths lack logging/metrics for rejected SQL expressions, so operators may see silent failures without visibility into attempted use

Double-checked locking is incomplete: after acquiring the write lock, the code does not re-check whether another goroutine has already populated entryPointAssetsCache, causing redundant reloads during concurrent cold starts

On error, the function unconditionally assigns entryPointAssetsCache = result where result may be nil, which can overwrite and clear a previously valid cache entry and force repeated reloads thereafter
Cleanup ticker interval in pkg/services/cleanup/cleanup.go was reduced from 10 minutes to 1 minute (likely unintended debug leftover) and increases risk of DB lock contention/deadlocks
Six routine cleanup progress logs in pkg/services/annotations/annotationsimpl/xorm_store.go are incorrectly emitted at Error level on the happy path, polluting error logs and triggering false alerts
One of the happy-path Error logs includes an "err" field where err is guaranteed nil at that point
Happy-path logs include full ID slices ("ids", ids), which can cause log flooding and increased ingestion costs on large annotation tables
SQLite >999-parameter branch builds the IN(...) ID list via repeated fmt.Sprintf string concatenation in a loop, causing O(n^2) time/allocations
Cleanup interval is hardcoded (no config/feature flag/comment), removing an operator tuning knob; should be configurable (e.g., via grafana.ini) if changed
Async legacy write goroutines derive their context from the cancellable request context, so the context is canceled when the handler returns and legacy writes are silently dropped
Create: when d.Storage.Create returns an error, the code records the failure using recordLegacyDuration instead of recordStorageDuration, misattributing the error and emitting misleading metrics
Update: when d.Storage.Update returns an error, the code records the failure using recordLegacyDuration instead of recordStorageDuration, misattributing the error and emitting misleading metrics
Delete: recordStorageDuration is called with the object name passed as the kind label, creating unbounded Prometheus label cardinality
DeleteCollection: the async legacy goroutine records duration using recordStorageDuration instead of recordLegacyDuration, double-counting storage and never populating legacy metrics

Update: legacy update no longer wraps objInfo with an updateWrapper that returns the storage-persisted object, so Legacy recomputes the updated object from its own pre-image and can diverge from Storage
Delete: ctx is set with klog.NewContext(ctx, d.Log) instead of the enriched per-call logger, so downstream logging loses name/kind/method fields
Async legacy goroutines lack panic recovery; a panic inside the goroutine can crash the apiserver process
Tests: Mode 3 tests reference a package-scope prometheus registry variable p whose declaration may be missing, potentially breaking compilation
Tests: sharing a mutable package-level prometheus.Registry across (possibly parallel) tests can cause duplicate collector registration panics

Federated rule detection was removed in useAllRulerRuleAbilities (isFederatedRuleGroup import dropped and isFederated hardcoded to false), causing federated Mimir rule groups to be treated as mutable and enabling actions (Edit/Delete/Pause/etc.) that will fail on the backend

React key prop is missing for GrafanaRuleListItem in FilterView’s list rendering (previously GrafanaRuleLoader had key={key}), leading to React key warnings and unstable row identity/state across re-renders
Grafana rule detail links no longer include the returnTo query parameter in GrafanaRuleListItem, breaking navigation context so users can’t return to the exact filtered list/scroll position
TOCTOU race in CreateOrUpdateDevice: CountDevices check and subsequent INSERT/UPSERT are separate statements without transaction/locking, allowing concurrent requests to exceed device_limit

Authenticate now calls TagDevice synchronously on the request context, so slow/stalled DB operations can block anonymous authentication indefinitely (or until DB timeout), increasing latency and enabling DoS amplification
Authenticate now uses the request context for TagDevice, so client disconnect cancels ctx mid-write (INSERT/UPDATE), potentially leaving inconsistent state and generating cancellation errors
Panic recovery around TagDevice was removed, so a panic in the tagging path can crash the auth handler
updateDevice returns ErrDeviceLimitReached when rowsAffected==0, conflating device-not-found or stale-window update misses with actual device-limit enforcement, making auth behavior/logs incorrect
anonymousDeviceExpiration constant is duplicated in anonstore and api packages, creating two sources of truth that can drift and cause inconsistent behavior
When device_limit>0, CreateOrUpdateDevice performs an extra CountDevices COUNT(*) query on every anonymous request, adding a high-RPS DB round-trip and potential performance bottleneck

Frontend config type for anonymousDeviceLimit (number|undefined) and initialization to undefined drift from backend behavior (always a number, 0 when disabled), causing incorrect feature gating/consumer logic

I18n.fallbacks.ensure_loaded! will raise NoMethodError at runtime if any environment file still sets config.i18n.fallbacks = true, because Rails after_initialize overwrites I18n.fallbacks with I18n::Locale::Fallbacks (which lacks ensure_loaded!)
Background jobs/rake tasks set I18n.locale without calling I18n.fallbacks.ensure_loaded!, so fallback locales are never loaded outside web requests and translations may not fall back correctly

FallbackLocaleList does not implement the I18n::Locale::Fallbacks interface method defaults, so I18n::Backend::Fallbacks may call I18n.fallbacks.defaults and raise NoMethodError during translation fallback resolution

FallbackLocaleList#[] calls SiteSetting.default_locale.to_sym unconditionally, which will crash with NoMethodError if SiteSetting.default_locale is nil during boot/tests or when settings are unavailable
traceID is no longer included in the "Plugin Request Completed" log line after switching to ContextualLoggerMiddleware, potentially breaking trace correlation
instrumentContext in ContextualLoggerMiddleware does not attach traceID to the context, so downstream logs may lose trace correlation unless infra logging adds it automatically
grafanaInfraLogWrapper.FromContext silently falls back to d.New() when the underlying logger type assertion fails, wiping all contextual attributes without any warning/metric
TestLogger.FromContext in pkg/plugins/log/fake.go returns a new disconnected logger instance, so tests inspecting f.InfoLogs/f.ErrorLogs can miss logs emitted via the FromContext path

No tests were added for ContextualLoggerMiddleware to verify it calls instrumentContext and attaches pluginId/endpoint/dsName/dsUID/uname for the relevant endpoints, allowing regressions to slip through CI
Renamed metrics middleware test file still uses the stale test function name TestInstrumentationMiddleware, creating inconsistency with the new MetricsMiddleware naming
SQL injection risk in migration due to interpolating admin-writable site_settings.value directly into raw INSERT SQL
Migration breaks on non-malicious host values containing single quotes because of unsafe SQL string interpolation
Admin embeddable_hosts_controller uses where(...).first and then calls destroy/save on nil, causing NoMethodError/500 instead of returning 404 for invalid ids
Controller lacks strong parameters and directly reads params[:embeddable_host][:host] without require/permit or nil-checking the parent key
Authorization may be insufficient: ensure_staff may be too weak for managing the embed allowlist; admin-only access should be considered
EmbeddableHost model before_validation calls host.sub! unconditionally, raising NoMethodError when host is nil
Frontend store hydration calls self.pluralize(...) but pluralize is undefined on the store object, causing runtime TypeError and breaking _ids hydration
Frontend store hydration calls obj[k].map(...) without guarding for null/undefined obj[k], causing TypeError when *_ids is null/undefined
Migration uses create_table force:true, which can drop existing embeddable_hosts data on migration re-run/redo

Fabricator definitions are in the wrong files (category_fabricator.rb contains embeddable_host and embeddable_host_fabricator.rb contains category fabricators), creating a maintainability trap and potential duplicate-fabricator load errors
record_for_host does not normalize hostname case before querying lower(host), causing allowlist mismatches for mixed/upper-case hosts
Host validation regex incorrectly caps TLD length at 5 characters, rejecting valid modern TLDs
Host validation regex permits path suffixes in stored host values, which could become an XSS risk if reflected unsanitized
Host validation regex does not reject @ userinfo components, allowing potentially unsafe/invalid host formats

In app/assets/stylesheets/desktop/topic-post.scss:291, the light-theme color for `.reply-count a` was changed from `scale-color($primary, $lightness: 30%)` to `scale-color($primary, $lightness: 70%)`, unintentionally lightening the link in light mode

In app/assets/stylesheets/desktop/topic-post.scss:291, the dark-theme arm uses `scale-color($secondary, $lightness: 30%)` but should be the inverted value `70%` (100-30) to match the intended dark-theme mapping
In app/assets/stylesheets/mobile/modal.scss:102, the `dark-light-choose` arguments for `.custom-message-length` are inverted, causing light mode to use `$primary` at 30% instead of the original 70%
In app/assets/stylesheets/desktop/user.scss:523, the light-theme color for `.name` was changed from `$primary` lightness 30% to 50%, removing the intended visual hierarchy vs `.title`
In app/assets/stylesheets/mobile/topic-post.scss:183, the light-theme color for `h3` was changed from `$primary` lightness 20% to 50%, significantly lightening headings in light mode
In app/assets/stylesheets/mobile/user.scss:498, the light-theme color for `.name` was changed from `$primary` lightness 30% to 50%, making it visually identical to `.title` in light mode
GroupsController#members accepts an unbounded `limit` parameter, enabling a potential DoS by requesting extremely large page sizes
Admin GroupsController `add_members`/`remove_member` mutate membership rows before `group.save`, so failures can return an error while the DB changes persist (non-transactional write flow)
Admin UI `totalPages` calculation is off-by-one when `user_count` is an exact multiple of `limit`, causing an extra empty page
`findMembers` returns `undefined` when group name is empty instead of a promise, breaking callers that chain `.then(...)`
Breaking API parameter shape changes for group `create`/`update` (top-level params vs `params[:group]`) are unversioned and can break external consumers
GroupsController#members response shape changed from a bare array to `{members, meta}` without versioning/deprecation, potentially breaking external consumers
Removed `/admin/groups/:id/users` route may still be used by plugins/themes/external code, causing silent 404s without a deprecation path or compatibility alias

Missing tests for new/changed behaviors: non-automatic group update with top-level params, add_members with nonexistent usernames, remove_member when user isn’t a member, and members pagination metadata across pages
Nitpick: early-return in group model uses `return };` (ASI-dependent) instead of idiomatic `return; }`, inconsistent with file style
Non-atomic read-modify-write of BlockedEmail.match_count in should_block? can lose increments under concurrent requests (race condition)
should_block? ignores the return value of record.save, potentially swallowing failed persistence of match_count/last_match_at updates

should_block? is called from EmailValidator#validate_each and can run multiple times per registration attempt (multiple valid?/save calls), causing match_count to be double-counted and breaking counter semantics
Email validation builds a regex from SiteSetting by escaping only '.', allowing unescaped metacharacters that can enable ReDoS via catastrophic backtracking on signup
Email validation regex construction can raise RegexpError for misconfigured settings (e.g., '*.example.com'), causing signup requests to error/DoS until fixed

Frontend create_account_controller pushes result.values.email without checking it exists, potentially adding undefined to rejectedEmails and causing spurious invalid state when accountEmail is undefined/empty
User profile template binds to `model.website_name` but Discourse camelizes serializer fields to `model.websiteName`, so the website name will render as empty/undefined
Ruby serializer uses mutating concatenation (`"." << website_host`) on a string literal, which can raise `FrozenError` under `# frozen_string_literal: true` and is semantically misleading
`URI(website.to_s)` is parsed multiple times per call and only the first parse is rescue-guarded; later `.path` accesses are unguarded and can raise on malformed URIs

Docstring annotation in `user.js` was changed from `@property websiteName` to `@property profileBackground`, potentially removing/misleading documentation for an existing `websiteName` computed property
Using Ruby open-uri/Kernel#open on untrusted URLs enables SSRF and potential command execution (e.g., leading '|' or internal IPs) in feed polling and remote import paths
RSS/embed content is stored and rendered as raw HTML via a new raw_html cook_method that bypasses sanitization, enabling stored XSS
embed.html.erb uses request.referer as postMessage targetOrigin (invalid because it’s a full URL, not an origin), causing the handshake to fail
request.referer is interpolated into a single-quoted JS string without escaping in embed.html.erb, allowing JS injection via a crafted Referer header
Parent-side postMessage origin validation uses indexOf substring matching instead of strict origin equality, allowing spoofed origins to pass the check
Embed access control relies on Referer host matching, which is suppressible/spoofable and not a reliable authorization mechanism
Setting X-Frame-Options to ALLOWALL is invalid/unsupported, so framing behavior is not reliably enforced
poll_feed.rb calls i.content.scrub when i.content may be nil for feeds without <content>, causing NoMethodError and aborting processing
Redis throttling uses SETNX followed by a separate EXPIRE, risking a permanent key with no TTL if a crash/failover occurs between commands
Migration uses create_table force: true, which can drop existing data when migrations are replayed/reset
Concurrent imports can race on embed_url creation, causing RecordNotUnique, returning 500s, and potentially leaving orphan topics
embed/best.html.erb contains '<%- end if %>' which is a Ruby syntax error and prevents the template from rendering
Duplicate OptimizedImage.downsize method definitions cause Ruby to silently override the legacy 4-argument signature, breaking existing callers and potentially crashing when opts is an Integer
Server-side remote URL download limit is hardcoded to 10MB, bypassing admin-configured SiteSetting.max_image_size_kb and enabling larger-than-configured downloads
Client-side upload size checks are hardcoded to 10MB, ignoring admin max_<type>_size_kb/max_image_size_kb settings and producing incorrect 413 messaging while ignoring the upload type

OptimizedImage.downsize now accepts an unvalidated dimensions string that flows into a backtick-executed ImageMagick command, widening shell-injection/RCE risk if user-controlled input ever reaches it
Upload downsize loop lacks convergence/failure checks (doesn’t break on no progress or convert failure), so it may exhaust attempts and still proceed with an oversized file
Downsizing in-place by using the same tempfile.path as both source and destination risks file corruption/0-byte output and unreliable size checks; should write to a separate temp file then rename

Invoking ImageMagick convert up to 5 times per upload increases resource-exhaustion and ImageTragick attack surface; should validate image safety (MIME/pixels) and enforce ImageMagick policy limits/disable risky coders
Unsubscribe action uses TopicUser.find_by which can return nil for users without a TopicUser row, causing NoMethodError when accessing tu.notification_level and calling tu.save!
Unsubscribe endpoint is a GET route that mutates server state, making it vulnerable to CSRF and accidental triggering by link previewers/URL scanners
Locale string requires %{unsubscribe_url} interpolation, causing I18n::MissingInterpolationArgument for mailers/callers that enable add_unsubscribe_link but do not pass unsubscribe_url
dropdown-button.js.es6 concatenates unescaped title into HTML, enabling stored XSS via user-controlled topic titles
unsubscribe.hbs uses triple-brace {{{stopNotificiationsText}}} to render an I18n string that interpolates an unescaped topic title, enabling XSS
Adding render :show inside shared perform_show_response forces all callers to render the show template, creating confusing coupling and breaking future actions’ rendering expectations

Unsubscribe action does not check topic authorization (guardian.ensure_can_see!) and lacks a nil-guard for missing topics, enabling private-topic existence probing and undefined behavior for invalid topic_id

`SelectedCalendarRepository.updateManyByCredentialId(this.credential.id, {})` passes empty update data, making `updateMany` a no-op so Prisma `@updatedAt` does not bump and the cache-last-updated indicator never refreshes
`scripts/test-gcal-webhooks.sh` uses BSD/macOS `sed -i '' -E` syntax that fails on GNU sed/Linux, preventing the script from updating the `.env` file in typical CI environments

`connectedCalendars.handler.ts` directly instantiates `new CalendarCacheRepository()` instead of using the feature-flagged mock, causing DB access (and possible runtime errors) even when the calendar-cache feature is disabled and violating the repository abstraction pattern

`deleteCache.handler.ts` authorization check only matches `{ id: credentialId, userId: user.id }`, missing team-shared and delegation credentials, enabling IDOR/broken access control where users can delete cache for credentials they should not control
`connectedCalendars.handler.ts` uses `cacheStatusMap.get(...) || null`, which incorrectly coerces any falsy value to null; should use `??` to only default on null/undefined
Working-hours boundary check computes both start and end from slotStartTime, ignoring slotEndTime, so slots that extend past workingHour.endTime can be incorrectly marked available
Day-off override check compares two Dayjs instances with === (reference equality), so the branch never triggers and blocked days remain bookable
utcOffset is multiplied by -1, inverting the timezone shift direction and causing date override matching to use the wrong local day
DST boundary bug: UTC offset sampled only at override.start is reused for override.end, potentially off by ±60 minutes across DST transitions
deleteMany cleanup predicate deletes reminders with retryCount > 1 without filtering method=SMS, potentially purging EMAIL/WHATSAPP reminders

If Twilio send succeeds but the subsequent Prisma update fails, the catch path increments retryCount without marking scheduled/persisting sid, causing the SMS to be resent on the next cron tick (duplicate SMS/charges)
Prisma update inside the catch block is unguarded; if it throws, the exception escapes and aborts the remaining cron loop iterations and may prevent sending the HTTP response
retryCount is incremented via non-atomic read-modify-write (retryCount: reminder.retryCount + 1), which can lose increments under concurrent cron runs; should use atomic increment
Retry deletion threshold uses gt: 1, creating ambiguous/off-by-one retry semantics; should clarify intended attempts and use a named constant/appropriate comparator (gte/gt)

retryCount column is global on WorkflowReminder but only SMS handler uses it; email/whatsapp handlers don’t update/clean it, creating divergent semantics and future misuse risk (consider smsRetryCount or extending pattern)
Exhausted reminders are silently deleted with no logging/metrics, reducing observability into failed deliveries
Logging raw Twilio error objects via console.log may leak sensitive data (phone numbers/message bodies) into logs

Public InsightsBookingService.findMany() method was removed and replaced with getBaseConditions() without documenting/updating all external call sites, risking compile-time breaks and forcing callers to reimplement where-composition safely
Constructor public options type allows scope:"team" without teamId, but the internal zod discriminated union requires teamId for team scope, causing runtime ZodError for calls that compile
Caching test suite (describe("Caching"...)) was deleted while caching fields/guards remain in production code, reducing coverage and allowing memoization regressions to go undetected

Tests assert Prisma.Sql fragments with toEqual(Prisma.sql`...`), which is fragile because it depends on Prisma.Sql internal structure that may change across Prisma versions; assertions may fail or become flaky without normalizing rendered SQL/params
Webhook handler trusts body-supplied userId, allowing any caller with the webhook secret to overwrite any user’s stored OAuth credentials (IDOR/cross-user credential overwrite)

Webhook decrypt uses `process.env.CALCOM_APP_CREDENTIAL_ENCRYPTION_KEY || ""`, so an unset encryption key falls back to empty string and enables trivial/known-key decryption of attacker-crafted ciphertexts
Webhook secret check can be bypassed when `CALCOM_WEBHOOK_SECRET` is unset because `undefined !== undefined` is false, allowing unauthenticated requests if both secret and header are missing
Webhook secret is compared with a non-constant-time `!==` check, enabling timing attacks to recover the secret (especially without rate limiting)
`parseRefreshTokenResponse` overwrites missing provider refresh tokens with the literal string "refresh_token", corrupting stored credentials and breaking future refreshes

`refreshOAuthTokens` returns a raw `fetch` Response object on the sync path (no `.json()`/status check), but callers pass it to `parseRefreshTokenResponse`, causing refresh parsing failures/crashes or credential corruption
Webhook endpoint lacks an HTTP method guard, so non-POST verbs can reach decrypt/upsert logic and may leak secrets via logs/caches
Webhook decrypt + `JSON.parse` are unguarded; malformed ciphertext/non-JSON causes 500s with distinguishable errors, creating an oracle and enabling DoS via crafted payloads
Credential-writing webhook has no rate limiting, making timing attacks and request-flood DoS practical
Credential-writing webhook has no tight body-size cap; large bodies can amplify DoS and exceed what’s needed for this endpoint
`CALCOM_CREDENTIAL_SYNC_ENDPOINT` is fetched without protocol/host validation, creating SSRF risk and allowing attacker-controlled token JSON to be persisted if misconfigured
When credential sync is enabled but `userId` is null, `refreshOAuthTokens` silently falls back to direct refresh with no logging, hiding sync bypass/failures
`minimumTokenResponseSchema` omits `refresh_token` but code mutates `.data.refresh_token`, making the returned type inaccurate and potentially causing TS/type-safety issues

`deleteScheduledEmailReminder` with `immediateDelete` cancels the SendGrid scheduled send but returns without deleting/updating the corresponding `WorkflowReminder` DB row, leaving orphaned/stale reminders after reschedule

In `scheduleEmailReminders.ts`, a single `client.request` failure inside a loop wrapped by one outer try/catch aborts the entire cancellation batch, skipping remaining reminders and skipping the trailing `Promise.all` for DB deletes/updates

In `handleCancelBooking.ts`, reminder-cancellation helpers are called inside synchronous `.forEach` without `await`, discarding returned promises so the handler can finish before DB writes complete and errors can become unhandled rejections

The same fire-and-forget (missing `await`/`Promise.all`) reminder-cancellation pattern is duplicated in `packages/trpc/server/routers/viewer/bookings.tsx` and multiple call sites in `packages/trpc/server/routers/viewer/workflows.tsx`

In `handleNewBooking.ts`, wrapping `forEach(async ...)` in `try/catch` cannot catch async rejections because `forEach` doesn’t await callbacks, making the outer catch ineffective and allowing unhandled rejections (or dead code if helpers swallow errors)
Permission check incorrectly computes isTeamAdminOrOwner using logical AND instead of OR, making the admin/owner authorization branch effectively unreachable
Guest email blacklist check is case-sensitive, allowing blacklisted addresses to bypass the blacklist via mixed-case input
Attendee deduplication compares emails case-sensitively, allowing duplicate attendee rows when the same email is provided with different casing

Email sending uses the raw guests list instead of the filtered uniqueGuests list, causing emails to be sent to recipients that should have been filtered out (e.g., existing attendees or blacklisted addresses)

Email manager logic can send the wrong template (scheduled/ICS invite) to existing attendees when their address is re-submitted, due to passing unfiltered guests and matching newGuests.includes(attendee.email)
AddGuests input schema has no maximum guest count (and no rate limiting), enabling abuse such as email-bombing and outbound mail queue/resource exhaustion

Email-send failures are swallowed: the handler logs with console.log without including the error and still returns success, leading to silent notification failures and inconsistent state (DB updated but no invites sent)
Organizer email subject may render an empty attendee name because it uses calEvent.attendees[0].name, which can be a newly added guest with name set to an empty string

Authorization allows any attendee to add guests without organizer consent, enabling unauthorized guest additions and potential leakage of event details (including video-call URL/password) to added recipients
Backup codes are stored using reversible symmetric encryption instead of being hashed (should store only password-grade KDF hashes like bcrypt/argon2/scrypt)
Backup-code consumption is not atomic (TOCTOU): concurrent login requests can reuse the same backup code and create multiple authenticated sessions
Backup-code verification uses non-constant-time comparison via Array.indexOf, leaking timing information about match position/prefix
React state for backupCodes is initialized with useState([]) and inferred as never[], erasing type safety for string[] backup codes
resetState() does not clear sensitive React state (backupCodes, backupCodesUrl, dataUri, secret), leaving plaintext credentials in memory after modal close/cancel
Blob URL created for downloading backup codes is not revoked on close/unmount, causing memory leak and prolonged exposure of plaintext backup codes via an accessible object URL
EventManager dereferences mainHostDestinationCalendar.integration without guarding when destinationCalendar is empty/null, causing a runtime TypeError crash

GoogleCalendarService updateEvent uses a tautological fallback find predicate comparing cal.externalId to a falsy externalCalendarId, so selectedCalendar becomes undefined and calendar.events.update is called with calendarId undefined

GoogleCalendarService deleteEvent repeats the same tautological fallback find predicate, so calendarId becomes undefined and calendar.events.delete is called with calendarId undefined (events on non-primary calendars not deleted)
LarkCalendarService.createEvent signature is missing the new required credentialId parameter, violating the updated Calendar interface and preventing per-credential destinationCalendar selection
Office365CalendarService.createEvent signature is missing the new required credentialId parameter, violating the updated Calendar interface and preventing per-credential destinationCalendar selection

BaseCalendarService.createEvent signature is missing the new required credentialId parameter, breaking subclasses (e.g., CalDAV/Apple) that inherit it and preventing per-credential destinationCalendar selection

handleNewBooking initializes evt.destinationCalendar to null in some cases; the COLLECTIVE branch then does evt.destinationCalendar?.push(...), which becomes a silent no-op and drops team destination calendars when organizer has no personal destinationCalendar

EventManager updateAllCalendarEvents may call updateEvent with an undefined credential after a DB fallback that doesn’t populate credential when app.slug is missing/uninstalled, causing updates to silently no-op
handleCancelBooking credential DB refetch omits including app.slug and mapping to appName, so downstream code sees appName undefined (breaking logs/emails/metrics)
handleNewBooking loadUsers dynamic-user branch no longer selects organization.slug, causing user.organization?.slug to be undefined downstream for dynamic bookings
handleNewBooking loadUsers host branch can return undefined instead of an array when there are no hosts, leading to crashes in callers that assume an array (e.g., slice/push)

Async callbacks passed to Array.prototype.forEach in the tRPC reschedule path (packages/trpc/server/routers/viewer/bookings.tsx:553) are not awaited, so calendar.deleteEvent runs after the mutation returns and errors become unhandled promise rejections

Async callbacks passed to Array.prototype.forEach in vital reschedule (packages/app-store/vital/lib/reschedule.ts:125) are not awaited, so deletions are fire-and-forget and errors bypass the enclosing try/catch

Async callbacks passed to Array.prototype.forEach in wipemycalother reschedule (packages/app-store/wipemycalother/lib/reschedule.ts:125) are not awaited, so deletions are fire-and-forget and errors bypass the enclosing try/catch

Recurring-event deletion in handleCancelBooking still uses forEach(async ...) over credentials (packages/features/bookings/lib/handleCancelBooking.ts:460), so deletions aren’t included in Promise.all(apiDeletes) and failures are silently swallowed

getCalendarCredentials constructs items with calendar: getCalendar(credential) (packages/core/CalendarManager.ts:28), so the calendar field becomes a Promise<Calendar|null> and consumers may treat it as Calendar|null (truthy checks/method calls) leading to subtle runtime bugs

Dynamic import() calls stored directly in the app-store barrel object (packages/app-store/index.ts:1) execute eagerly at module initialization, defeating intended lazy loading and adding unnecessary await overhead to lookups

getVideoAdapters was rewritten to await appStore imports sequentially in a for...of loop (packages/core/videoClient.ts:21), reducing parallelism and increasing latency when multiple video credentials are present

`fetch_error_details` pairs `error_ids` with `events.values()` from `nodestore.backend.get_multi`, relying on dict value ordering that may not match `node_ids`, potentially associating wrong event data with an error
`test_get_with_error` only covers a single error event, so it cannot detect mis-ordering/reshuffle bugs in `fetch_error_details`

Environment filter (`environment: selection.environments`) was removed from the `/trace-items/attributes/` query; if the backend previously honored it, results will now incorrectly merge attribute keys across environments

`owner` actor for workflow engine detector is accepted from the request and written to `owner_user_id`/`owner_team_id` without explicit same-organization validation, risking cross-org actor reference (IDOR-style issue) if `ActorField` is not scoped

`schedule_webhook_delivery` uses a 30s `processing_deadline_duration`, which may be too short under load for the scheduler pass and could cause timeouts/pages; should likely be increased (e.g., to 120s) and measured

Default project option `sentry:autofix_automation_tuning` changed from `"low"` to `"off"` without migration/release note/flag, potentially disabling autofix for projects that never explicitly set the option

`feedback_summaries.make_input_prompt` changed from a bulleted list to joining with `------`, altering LLM prompt structure and risking delimiter collisions when user feedback contains `------`; delimiter should be escaped or made collision-resistant and evals updated
PR description is `Test 5`, providing no meaningful intent/scope for reviewers to validate changes against
PR mixes many unrelated subsystems in one diff (scope creep), making review and rollback difficult; should be split into smaller per-area PRs

Migrations `0917` and `0920` were changed to early `return` and reference `0921_convert_org_saved_searches_to_views_rerevised.py`, but that migration file is not included; merge could break the migration chain if it doesn't already exist on main
